rationale: The proposed homestead exemption increase under Tax Code §11.13(b) has no relation to Gov't Code §2001.903, which governs attorney's fee recovery in frivolous regulatory contested cases. No overlap or conflict exists.
quote: The administrative law judge in a contested case may award a person, in addition to all other costs allowed by law or rule, an amount not to exceed $1 million for reasonable attorney's fees and costs incurred in defending against a frivolous regulatory action during the case
_note: Rule text concerns ALJ attorney's fee awards in contested cases; proposed change concerns property tax homestead exemption. No intersection.
